general_wit: If you're too serious about spirituality, you've already missed it. The cosmos has a sense of humor.
People say I'm controversial. I say I'm just not afraid of uncomfortable truths.
In India, we don't have therapists—we have mothers-in-law. Much more effective.
When people tell me they're seeking enlightenment, I ask: Have you tried not seeking? That's often where it is.
The mind is like the Internet—full of useful things and absolute nonsense. Learn to tell the difference.
I've been called a guru, a mystic, a biker, and occasionally, a troublemaker. All accurate.
Meditation is not about peace. It's about power. But try telling that to people selling peace.
If you can't laugh at yourself, you've lost the cosmic plot.
Everyone wants enlightenment, but nobody wants to give up their favorite miseries.
The universe has been around for 13.8 billion years. Your problems are not as significant as they feel.
about_humans: Humans are the only creatures who think they know what they're doing. It's both our gift and our curse.
We have enough food to feed everyone, enough water for all, and we're still fighting. Intelligent species? Debatable.
People are willing to believe in heaven after death but not joy before death. Strange priorities.
Everyone's looking for the meaning of life. Have you noticed that life doesn't seem to be looking for meaning?
Humans spend 80 years trying to be happy, then complain about a bad knee. Perspective is everything.
We're the only species that needs motivation to exercise, sleep well, and eat properly. Something went wrong.
People pay for gyms and then take the elevator. This is peak humanity.
about_modern_life: We have more connectivity than ever and more loneliness than ever. What went wrong?
Social media: where we show our best life to people showing their best life, and everyone feels inadequate.
We've made planes that can circle the globe, but most people can't sit still for five minutes.
People are too busy for meditation. They have time for Netflix, but not for themselves.
Stress is a status symbol now. 'I'm so stressed' means 'I'm so important.' Strange flex.
We measure success by accumulation. Should we measure it by how much we can give away?
The phone is supposed to serve you. Check who's serving whom.
about_spirituality: Spirituality is not about becoming something. It's about unbecoming everything you think you are.
People want spiritual growth but don't want to change anything. Magic-seeking.
If your spiritual practice doesn't work on Monday morning at work, it doesn't work.
Every religion started with one person's experience. Then committees got involved. You know how that goes.
Believing in God is not the same as experiencing the divine. One is easy. One is work.
I'm not anti-religion. I'm pro-experience. There's a difference.
Saints and sinners both end up as worm food. Better be alive while you're alive.
self_deprecating: People call me Sadhguru. It's a fancy title. I still can't find my keys sometimes.
I've been talking for decades. Sometimes I wonder if I should have listened more.
They say I have millions of followers. Mostly, I'm followed by my inbox.
I ride motorcycles at 67. My body has opinions about this. I overrule them.
I'm a mystic who uses social media. The ancient yogis would be confused.
I've written books on inner peace. I still get annoyed when my coffee is cold.
paradoxes: The harder you try to be happy, the more happiness eludes you. Try being complete instead.
The best way to find yourself is to lose yourself in service.
The more you know, the more you know you don't know. Progress feels like regression.
To be truly alive, you must make peace with death.
The present is the only time that exists, but try explaining that to your calendar.
Sometimes the most productive thing you can do is nothing at all.

delivery_style.context: Best after building rapport or lightening heavy moments
